outcomes.detail: Synchronous char-vector query frames are byte-identical.
The same primitive payload and supported grid/qText choices were compared.

outcomes.rationale: SQLTools retains legacy table/text aliases. Standalone accepts only its KX-owned grid/qText core contract and normalizes UI settings at its own panel boundary.

outcomes.area: exact selection and physical current line
outcomes.detail: Selections, whitespace, CRLF lines, and clamped cursor positions use the same exact-text contract.
Reference exposes currentQBlock/selectedTextOrCurrentBlock; standalone intentionally preserves current-line behavior but has not resolved the separate optional block command.
outcomes.action: Make an explicit first-party product decision: implement a standalone q-block helper/command with tests, or formally exclude it and reclassify this row by design.
outcomes.signoff: The reviewed decision is documented and the executable case asserts the resulting standalone contract.

outcomes.detail: Both public query wrappers preserve root-namespace q text byte-for-byte.

outcomes.rationale: Standalone owns strict-root Server Explorer execution and a .Q.ld script wrapper; SQLTools exposes its legacy raw namespace wrapper through the driver. Semantic common behavior is tested live below.

outcomes.detail: The same rows, selection, grouping, validation, and sampling request were executed by both pure engines.

Exact export text, escaping, headers, row index, nested values, and line breaks were compared.
Exact export text, escaping, headers, row index, nested values, and line breaks were compared.

Header/index expansion at Excel row and column limits produced identical validation results.
Private test adapters generated the same unzipped OOXML entries; ZIP timestamps/compression bytes were excluded.
Both direct clients rejected a closed loopback endpoint as a phase-bearing IPC error without query data.
A TCP peer that never completes q IPC handshake produced the same phase/timeout class and closed cleanly.

outcomes.area: direct IPC query timeout and error anonymity
outcomes.detail: A stalled issued query timed out in the query phase, destroyed the uncertain transport, and omitted q source text from errors.
An encoded q error stayed KdbQError and the same healthy socket served the following query.

outcomes.area: direct IPC timeout and diagnostics ownership
outcomes.rationale: Standalone owns split connect/query deadlines plus a KX OutputChannel/redaction schema. The pinned SQLTools adapter retains one timeoutMs and console performance tracing.

outcomes.detail: Both loopback servers exercised valid/invalid tokens, methods, endpoints, metadata, current/slice/selection CSV/JSON/NDJSON, headers, ranges, and limits.

outcomes.rationale: The same no_current_result protocol code is retained, while user-facing text names the owning KX result versus the SQLTools kdb panel.

The standalone .Q.ld wrapper succeeds; the pinned reference raw value wrapper raises a genuine q error for the same multiline source.
outcomes.action: Backport a compatible q-native script-grouping adapter to the SQLTools driver without changing its UI/session ownership.
outcomes.signoff: The shared LF and CRLF script fixtures return 30 and 5 through both adapters and restore the root namespace after success/error.

outcomes.detail: The standalone manifest/lock version stayed aligned and no SQLTools runtime, extension dependency, command, configuration, or source import entered the KX package.

outcomes.rationale: Standalone owns the KX sidebar/form, SecretStorage, focused Server Explorer, Query History, and one KX result target. Reference owns SQLTools driver/session/UI targets and its extension dependency.

outcomes.detail: Reference has test/e2e; standalone has deterministic host-free tests but no test/e2e directory or Extension Host workflow.
outcomes.action: Add a standalone Extension Host suite for activation, KX connection tree/form/SecretStorage, commands, result-panel protocol, cancellation, and settings without importing SQLTools.
outcomes.signoff: The suite runs in CI and locally on a supported VS Code build from a clean standalone commit.

outcomes.area: VS Code Extension Host and visual/manual UX
outcomes.rationale: Neither code nor code-insiders is installed, and this repository has no truthful cross-extension visual browser/Extension Host fixture. Source and webview parsing are not visual evidence.
outcomes.signoff: Record supported VS Code runs for activation, theme/layout, virtual scrolling, selection/keyboard, chart zoom/reset, settings, error states, and screenshots where useful.

outcomes.rationale: The shared fixture is deliberately anonymous and loopback-only; no authenticated endpoint was available or invented.
outcomes.signoff: Run accepted and rejected credentials against an authorized real q endpoint and verify SecretStorage/error redaction without recording credentials.

outcomes.rationale: Only anonymous IPv4 loopback direct q was authorized. No SSH/TLS service, remote host, IPv6 listener, or multi-version q matrix was available.
outcomes.signoff: Record separately authorized endpoint tests for every supported transport/address/q-version claim; keep standalone direct-only unless product scope changes.

outcomes.rationale: The gate proves OOXML ZIP structure and limits but no Excel/LibreOffice GUI application was available for visual rendering.
outcomes.signoff: Open representative exports in supported spreadsheet applications and record data, escaping, dimensions, and limits.

outcomes.rationale: This is not a feature release; the gate intentionally does not create a user-facing VSIX, install from Marketplace, or publish.
outcomes.signoff: Use a separately authorized release gate for package inventory, clean Extension Host installation, identity, credentials, hashes, and Marketplace upload.

outcomes.rationale: Both public products document local wait/transport cancellation limits; the harness does not claim reliable interruption of already-dispatched q work.
outcomes.signoff: Define an authorized server interruption protocol and prove side-effect/cancellation semantics before claiming server-side cancellation.

outcomes.detail: Current standalone documentation explicitly denies complete functional/visual parity and defers source-of-truth sign-off.
